# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains large-scale empirical evidence comparing merged agentic and human GitHub pull requests on structural change metrics and description-to-diff alignment metrics, along with method details and stated validity limits.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: method
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, Step 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The AIDev dataset used in this study contains 932,791 agentic PRs and 6,618 human PRs across 116,211 repositories.

## Source Excerpt

"Step 1: Dataset Collection. We use the AIDev dataset ... The dataset contains 932,791 Agentic PRs and 6,618 Human PRs across 116,211 repositories"

## Note

This describes the starting dataset before filtering for the final analysis set.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: method
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, Step 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The human PRs in the AIDev dataset lacked commit-level information, so the authors retrieved commit metadata and file-level patches for all human PRs using the GitHub REST API.

## Source Excerpt

"Unlike Agentic PRs, the Human PRs in the AIDev dataset lack commit-level information. To enable consistent comparison, we retrieved commit metadata and file-level patches for all Human PRs using the GitHub REST API"

## Note
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: method
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, Step 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Files touched were computed as unique file paths modified across all commits in a PR, and GitHub-reported file renames were treated as single-file modifications.

## Source Excerpt

"The number of files touched is computed as the set of unique file paths modified across all commits, ensuring repeated edits to the same file are counted once. File renames reported by GitHub are treated as single-file modifications."

## Note
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: method
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, Step 3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
After filtering, the analysis set contains 24,014 agentic PRs with 440,295 commits with valid patches and 5,081 human PRs with 23,242 commits with valid patches.

## Source Excerpt

"After filtering, the analysis set contains 24,014 Agentic PRs (440,295 commits with valid patches) and 5,081 Human PRs (23,242 commits with valid patches)."

## Note
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: method
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, Step 4
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The distributions for all structural metrics violated normality assumptions, so the study used the Mann-Whitney U test and quantified effect sizes with Cliff's delta.

## Source Excerpt

"Normality assumptions were violated for all metrics, motivating the use of the Mann–Whitney U test ... to assess differences between the two groups. Effect sizes were quantified with Cliff’s delta"

## Note
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: metric
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Commit count showed the largest reported effect size between agentic and human PRs, with Cliff's delta = 0.5429, labeled large.

## Source Excerpt

"Table 1. Cliff’s δ Effect Sizes - agentic vs. human PRs ... Commits 0.5429 Large"

## Note
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: metric
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Files touched differed between agentic and human PRs with Cliff's delta = 0.4487, labeled medium.

## Source Excerpt

"Table 1. Cliff’s δ Effect Sizes - agentic vs. human PRs ... Files Touched 0.4487 Medium"

## Note
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: metric
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Deletions differed between agentic and human PRs with Cliff's delta = 0.4462, labeled medium.

## Source Excerpt

"Table 1. Cliff’s δ Effect Sizes - agentic vs. human PRs ... Deletions 0.4462 Medium"

## Note
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: metric
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Additions differed between agentic and human PRs with Cliff's delta = 0.2836, labeled small.

## Source Excerpt

"Table 1. Cliff’s δ Effect Sizes - agentic vs. human PRs ... Additions 0.2836 Small"

## Note
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: metric
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Total line changes, defined as additions plus deletions, differed between agentic and human PRs with Cliff's delta = 0.3158, labeled small.

## Source Excerpt

"Table 1. Cliff’s δ Effect Sizes - agentic vs. human PRs ... Line Changes (add + + delete) 0.3158 Small"

## Note
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: result
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, RQ1 results
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The Mann-Whitney U test found statistically significant differences between agentic and human PRs across the structural metrics at p ≤ 0.001.

## Source Excerpt

"Overall, the result of the Mann–Whitney U test shows that the differences are statistically significant ( p ≤ 0.001 )"

## Note
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: metric
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, Table 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The mean TF-IDF description-to-diff similarity was 0.1245 for agentic PRs and 0.1007 for human PRs.

## Source Excerpt

"Table 2. Descriptive statistics for lexical and semantic similarity metrics ... Mean ... TF–IDF ... 0.1245 ... 0.1007"

## Note
```

### file: evidence-13.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-13
kind: metric
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, Table 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The mean CodeBERT similarity was 0.9356 for agentic PRs and 0.9285 for human PRs.

## Source Excerpt

"Table 2. Descriptive statistics for lexical and semantic similarity metrics ... Mean ... CodeBERT 0.9356 ... 0.9285"

## Note
```

### file: evidence-14.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-14
kind: metric
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, Table 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The mean GraphCodeBERT similarity was 0.8254 for agentic PRs and 0.7815 for human PRs.

## Source Excerpt

"Table 2. Descriptive statistics for lexical and semantic similarity metrics ... Mean ... GraphCodeBERT 0.8254 ... 0.7815"

## Note
```

### file: evidence-15.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-15
kind: method
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, Step 4
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Okapi BM25 is unbounded, can produce large positive or negative values on long token-heavy diffs, and should be interpreted only as a relative lexical signal rather than a calibrated similarity measure.

## Source Excerpt

"Okapi BM25, in contrast, is an unbounded relevance score whose magnitude depends on token frequencies and document length; long and token-heavy diffs can therefore produce very large positive or negative values ... As a result, Okapi BM25 should be interpreted only as a relative lexical signal rather than a calibrated similarity measure"

## Note

The source explicitly limits interpretability of this metric and flags score magnitude as unstable across diff length and tokenization.
```

### file: evidence-16.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-16
kind: incident
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, Threats to Validity
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The authors state that missing or truncated patches and GitHub API retrieval gaps may bias distributions, and very large diff truncation may affect similarity metrics.

## Source Excerpt

"Missing or truncated patches may bias the distributions ... GitHub API retrieval for Human PRs may introduce gaps due to rate limits, deleted repositories, or rewritten histories. Additionally, it may truncate diffs for very large changes, which can reduce patch completeness and may affect similarity metrics computed from diff text."

## Note

This is a stated validity limit on the completeness of patch data and the reliability of similarity estimates derived from diff text.
---

---

evidence_id: evidence-15

mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Okapi BM25 is unbounded, can produce large positive or negative values on long token-heavy diffs, and should be interpreted only as a relative lexical signal rather than a calibrated similarity measure.

## Source Excerpt

"Okapi BM25, in contrast, is an unbounded relevance score whose magnitude depends on token frequencies and document length; long and token-heavy diffs can therefore produce very large positive or negative values ... As a result, Okapi BM25 should be interpreted only as a relative lexical signal rather than a calibrated similarity measure"

## Note

The source explicitly limits interpretability of this metric and flags score magnitude as unstable across diff length and tokenization.
```

### file: evidence-16.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-09-005
evidence_id: evidence-16
kind: incident
citation: Ogenrwot and Businge, MSR 2026, Threats to Validity
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The authors state that missing or truncated patches and GitHub API retrieval gaps may bias distributions, and very large diff truncation may affect similarity metrics.

## Source Excerpt

"Missing or truncated patches may bias the distributions ... GitHub API retrieval for Human PRs may introduce gaps due to rate limits, deleted repositories, or rewritten histories. Additionally, it may truncate diffs for very large changes, which can reduce patch completeness and may affect similarity metrics computed from diff text."

## Note

This is a stated validity limit on the completeness of patch data and the reliability of similarity estimates derived from diff text.

# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains large-scale empirical evidence comparing merged agentic and human GitHub pull requests on structural change metrics and description-to-diff alignment metrics, along with method details and stated validity limits.

## Evidence Files

```markdown
---

The AIDev dataset used in this study contains 932,791 agentic PRs and 6,618 human PRs across 116,211 repositories.

"Step 1: Dataset Collection. We use the AIDev dataset ... The dataset contains 932,791 Agentic PRs and 6,618 Human PRs across 116,211 repositories"

This describes the starting dataset before filtering for the final analysis set.

```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1

confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The human PRs in the AIDev dataset lacked commit-level information, so the authors retrieved commit metadata and file-level patches for all human PRs using the GitHub REST API.

## Source Excerpt

"Unlike Agentic PRs, the Human PRs in the AIDev dataset lack commit-level information. To enable consistent comparison, we retrieved commit metadata and file-level patches for all Human PRs using the GitHub REST API"

## Note
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1

evidence_id: evidence-03

source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2601.17581
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Files touched were computed as unique file paths modified across all commits in a PR, and GitHub-reported file renames were treated as single-file modifications.

## Source Excerpt

"The number of files touched is computed as the set of unique file paths modified across all commits, ensuring repeated edits to the same file are counted once. File renames reported by GitHub are treated as single-file modifications."

## Note
```
